summary: Reporter gets CS0809 'Obsolete member overrides non-obsolete member' warning for OnSizeChanged in their custom CircularGauge class inheriting from SKCanvasView on Android after upgrading Xamarin.AndroidX packages, but SkiaSharp has never marked OnSizeChanged as [Obsolete].

stepsToReproduce: Create a custom class inheriting from SKCanvasView (CircularGauge)
Upgrade Xamarin.AndroidX.AppCompat to 1.2.0.7 and Navigation.Fragment to 2.3.3.1
Observe 'Obsolete member overrides non-obsolete member' warning on OnSizeChanged

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: SkiaSharp 2.80.x is obsolete; the current 3.x version does not mark OnSizeChanged as [Obsolete] and never has. The warning is almost certainly caused by the reporter's project configuration or inheritance chain, not SkiaSharp.

analysis.summary: The reporter's CircularGauge class gets CS0809 warning for OnSizeChanged after upgrading AndroidX packages. Code investigation confirms SKCanvasView.OnSizeChanged has never been marked [Obsolete] in any SkiaSharp version. The fact that only one of the reporter's two gauge classes is affected (CircularGauge but not SemiCircularGauge) strongly indicates this is a user-side project configuration issue, not a SkiaSharp defect.
analysis.rationale: Classified as question because: (1) OnSizeChanged is not and has never been [Obsolete] in SKCanvasView, (2) the issue is asymmetric — only one custom subclass is affected, (3) the timing correlates with a Xamarin.AndroidX upgrade, not a SkiaSharp change, (4) the reporter explicitly asks for suggestions, not reporting broken behavior. The area is SkiaSharp.Views since the question concerns SKCanvasView's Android platform implementation.

keySignals.text: However now only on the CircularGauge, I get the message in the heading for OnSizeChanged
keySignals.interpretation: Only one of two classes is affected — indicates user-side issue, not library defect.

keySignals.text: Using the SemiCircularGauge class if I type protected override – IntelliSense prompts you for all the overrides. But for the CircularGauge nothing.
keySignals.interpretation: Asymmetric behavior between two classes using the same base type suggests the CircularGauge class itself has a problem, not SKCanvasView.

keySignals.text: the method isn't marked as an obsolete/deprecated member in the first place
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly observes that OnSizeChanged shouldn't be obsolete — confirms this is not a SkiaSharp-originated attribute.
The timing coincides with an AndroidX package upgrade, suggesting the issue may be caused by binding metadata changes in the AndroidX packages.

codeInvestigation.finding: OnSizeChanged override has no [Obsolete] attribute. Method simply calls base.OnSizeChanged and updates canvas size. Confirmed in current code and pre-3.0 code (via git history).
The only [Obsolete] method in the 2.x-era SKCanvasView was OnDraw(SKSurface, SKImageInfo), not OnSizeChanged. This was removed in commit a140a2033 for SkiaSharp 3.0.

resolution.hypothesis: The CS0809 warning is not caused by SkiaSharp. The CircularGauge class likely has a different inheritance chain or project-level configuration issue introduced during the Xamarin.AndroidX upgrade. The asymmetry between CircularGauge and SemiCircularGauge confirms the problem is in the reporter's code.

proposals.title: Check CircularGauge inheritance chain
proposals.description: Verify that CircularGauge directly inherits from SKCanvasView and not from an intermediate class that marks OnSizeChanged as [Obsolete]. Compare the class declaration with SemiCircularGauge.

proposals.title: Clean and rebuild after AndroidX upgrade
proposals.description: Clean the bin/obj folders and rebuild the entire solution after the Xamarin.AndroidX package upgrade. Stale build artifacts can cause phantom warnings.

resolution.recommendedReason: The asymmetric behavior between two classes using the same SKCanvasView base is the strongest signal. Comparing their class declarations will almost certainly reveal the difference.

actionability.reason: This is a support question about a warning not caused by SkiaSharp. OnSizeChanged has never been [Obsolete] in any SkiaSharp version. The asymmetric behavior and AndroidX upgrade timing point to user-side configuration. No comments for 4+ years.

actions.description: Explain that OnSizeChanged has never been [Obsolete] in SkiaSharp and suggest checking the CircularGauge class hierarchy
actions.comment:
Thanks for the report. SkiaSharp's `SKCanvasView.OnSizeChanged` has never been marked `[Obsolete]` in any version, including 2.80.2. The fact that only your `CircularGauge` class shows this warning (while `SemiCircularGauge` does not) strongly suggests the issue is in the `CircularGauge` class hierarchy rather than in SkiaSharp.

Would you be able to compare the class declaration and inheritance chain of `CircularGauge` vs `SemiCircularGauge`? If `CircularGauge` inherits from an intermediate class (rather than directly from `SKCanvasView`), that intermediate class may be the source of the `[Obsolete]` attribute. Also, a clean rebuild (deleting `bin`/`obj` folders) after the Xamarin.AndroidX upgrade may help clear any stale build artifacts causing phantom warnings.

actions.description: Convert to discussion — support question not caused by SkiaSharp
